title: CRAC Unit Failure and Alarm State
spl:
index=cooling sourcetype="bms:crac"
| where unit_alarm=1 OR compressor_fault=1 OR fan_fault=1 OR unit_status="fault"
| table _time, unit_name, unit_alarm, compressor_fault, fan_fault, alarm_text
description: Explicit CRAC fault/alarm bits catch compressor and fan failures not visible in temperature alone.
value: Explicit CRAC fault/alarm bits catch compressor and fan failures not visible in temperature alone.
grandmaExplanation: Explicit CRAC fault/alarm bits catch compressor and fan failures not visible in temperature alone — so power, cooling, and physical access problems are found before they hurt uptime.
implementation: Map vendor alarm codes to plain text. Page on any fault. Track MTTR for CRAC repairs.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: BMS, SNMP (Liebert/Vertiv MIBs).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="bms:crac"` (unit_alarm, compressor_fault, fan_fault).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Map vendor alarm codes to plain text. Page on any fault. Track MTTR for CRAC repairs.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cooling sourcetype="bms:crac"
| where unit_alarm=1 OR compressor_fault=1 OR fan_fault=1 OR unit_status="fault"
| table _time, unit_name, unit_alarm, compressor_fault, fan_fault, alarm_text
```

Understanding this SPL

**CRAC Unit Failure and Alarm State** — Explicit CRAC fault/alarm bits catch compressor and fan failures not visible in temperature alone.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="bms:crac"` (unit_alarm, compressor_fault, fan_fault). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): BMS, SNMP (Liebert/Vertiv MIBs). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cooling; **sourcetype**: bms:crac. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cooling, sourcetype="bms:crac". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where unit_alarm=1 OR compressor_fault=1 OR fan_fault=1 OR unit_status="fault"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **CRAC Unit Failure and Alarm State**): table _time, unit_name, unit_alarm, compressor_fault, fan_fault, alarm_text


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Status grid (unit × fault class), Timeline (alarms), Single value (units in fault).
visualization: Status grid (unit × fault class), Timeline (alarms), Single value (units in fault).
